column1: 1,High Pressure Washing,each,980,700,"NOTE TO CLIENT: This is a noisy process and can be messy. This should not take more than the indicted days and will be cleaned up. Please point out water sources to be used to the Team Leader upon commencment of work.

column2: High pressure wash min pressure 200 bar
Wash to remove all salt and dirt + Loose material from area.
When working on the roof use safety harness and anchor point for safety.
Use drop sheet and garbage bags to collect all loose material generated.
Perform cleanup of the area before commencing to with next step"

column1: 2,Roof Wash,m²,12,60,"NOTE TO CLIENT: This is a noisy process and can be messy. This should not take more than the indicted days and will be cleaned up. Please point out water sources to be used to the Team Leader upon commencment of work.

column2: High pressure wash min pressure 200 bar
Apply 1 coat Midas FUNGICIDAL WASH to all affected areas.
Allow minimum 24 hours reaction time.
Remove all growth with a stiff fibre brush.
Apply SECOND coat of FUNGICIDAL WASH.
DO NOT rinse off the second coat.
Failure to follow this sequence risks regrowth.
When working on the roof use safety harness and anchor point for safety.
Use drop sheet and garbage bags to collect all loose material generated.
Perform cleanup of the area before commencing to with next step"
Remove ALL loose, defective and damaged plaster
Prime area with 1 coat bonding liquid
Repair with Paintsmiths PLASTER REPAIR KIT
Do not exceed 20mm thickness.
Do not rush curing â€“ insufficient curing leads to failure
Wet plaster 2 times daily or cover with dropsheet after first wetting.
Smoothen plaster or use a sponge to match existing texture."
Rake out all cracks wider than 0.5mm (not hairline cracks)
Prime with waterproofing slurry kit OR PCT36
Build up cracks with REPAIR MIX
Smoothen or use a sponge to match existing texture
Open all expansion joints and seal with All Round Sealer"
Remove damaged coating, loose materials or rotten wood.
Sand down reamining wood to uniform matt finish
Prime all replacement pieces of wood with Wood Primer
Spot prime nails, hinges and fittings with Metal Etch Primer
Treat knots or resin marks with Knotting and Wood sealer
Allow to dry and lightly sand to create a good surface for priming"
Remove all loose contaminents with a soft bristle brush
Spray Aluminium Cleaner, let soak for 5 to 10min and wipe off
Apply Alu Revivie to a soft fibre cloth and apply in circular motion till dry
Add coates of Alu revive till desired finish is achieved"
Apply 1 coat Midas FUNGICIDAL WASH to all affected areas.
Allow minimum 24 hours reaction time.
Remove all growth with a stiff fibre brush.
Apply SECOND coat of FUNGICIDAL WASH.
DO NOT rinse off the second coat.
Failure to follow this sequence risks regrowth."
Complete repairs first
Allow fillers and repairs to dry
Use a steel trowel and Exterior OR Interior Skimfill to achieve smooth uniform surface
Sand lightly and wipe down before continuing to prime and paint"
Ensure surfaces are clean inside and out.
Seal leaks and joints using All Round Sealer, use Peel and Seel for larger gaps
Apply 1 coat of Universal Primer
Apply 2 coats of Midalux 240 (colour to be secified)"
Preperation work quoted separately
Remove all loose contaminents and let dry after Pressure Wash.
Allow any repair work to fully dry
Sand lightly and remove dust
Spot prime repairs with Masonry Primer. On new build Prime entire wall with Masonry Primer
Apply 2 coats of Midalux 240 (colour to be specified)"
Preperation work quoted separately
Remove all loose contaminents and let dry after Pressure Wash.
Allow any repair work to fully dry
Sand lightly and remove dust
Spot prime repairs with Masonry Primer. On new build Prime entire wall with Masonry Primer
Apply 1 coat of Midamite Fine/Medium/Coarse to suit existing texture (colour to be specified)
Apply 2 coats of Midalux 240 (colour to be specified)"
Ensure roof is dry and clean. Do not paint in high humidity, temperature or probability of mist/rain.
Spot prime nails, roof screws and metal fittings with Rust Neutrelizer and Metal Etch Primer.
If needed apply 1 coat of Primer
Apply 2 coats of Midas Masteroof OR Rubberduck (colour to be specified)"
Clean metal with Midas Degreaser
Lighlty sand metal to dull uniform surface
Add caulk to frame and wall gaps
Apply 1 coat Metaletch Primer
Apply 1 coat Midaflow Gloss or Midas Masterroof (colour to be specified)"
If bare/ new wood present, apply Midas Woodprime to all new surfaces
Lightly sand to uniform colour/appearance
Add caulk to frame and wall gaps
Replace cracking/dry or missing putty. Use Putty Hardener
Spot prime nails, screws or metal fittings with Metal Etch Primer
Apply 1 coat of Universal Undercoat
Apply 2 coat of Midalux 240 OR 2 coats of Midaflow (ext) WB Non-Drip (int) (colour to be specified)"

Add caulk to frame and wall gaps

Add caulk to frame and wall gaps

Apply 1 coat of Indoor/Outdoor Varnish and let dry
Lightly sand and apply second coat of Indoor/Outdoor Varnish"

Apply 1 coat of 504 Surface Tolerant Epoxy

Apply 2 coats of 112 Solvent Based Acrithane Sealer"

Lighlty sand metal to dull uniform surface
Add caulk to frame and wall gaps
Apply 1 coat Metaletch Primer
Apply 1 coat Midaflow Gloss or Midas Masterroof (colour to be specified)"
If not skimmed/repaired wash walls with Sugar Soap where contaminated
Let repairs/wash completely dry
Sand repairs and spot prime with Plaster Primer. On new builds prime entire wall with Plaster Primer
Apply two coats of Midafelt 230 (Colour to be specified)"
If not skimmed/repaired wash walls with Sugar Soap where contaminated
Let repairs/wash completely dry
Sand repairs and spot prime with Plaster Primer
Apply 1 coat of Midamite Fine/Medium/Coarse to suite existing texture (colour to be specified)
Apply two coats of Midafelt 230 (Colour to be specified)"
Wash ceilings where necessary.
Remove all dust, cobwebs or loose contamination with soft bristle brush.
Caulk ceilings to cornice or wall.
For skimmed ceilings, prime with Midas Plaster Primer.
Apply 2coats of Midafelt 225 (colour to be specified)"
Remove loose contaminents and wash with Midas Degreaser where neccesary
Allow to fully dry, then sand lightly to remove gloss and get a uniform finish.
Caulk skirting where neccesary
Apply 1 coat of Universal Undercoat
Allow 4 hours for overcoating
Apply 2 coats of Midafelt 225 OR 2 coats of Waterbased Non-drip Enamel (colour to be specified)"

Allow to fully dry, then sand lightly to remove gloss and get a uniform finish.

Allow 4 hours for overcoating
Apply 2 coats of Midafelt 225 OR 2 coats of Waterbased Non-drip Enamel (colour to be specified)"
Remove all loose contaminants.
Apply 1 coat of Waterpoof Slurry Kit from the floor to +-/ 30cm above the affected area.
Wait 1 to 2 hours and apply second coat of Waterproof Slurry Kit to same area.
Continue with priming and painting."
Remove all loose contaminants and materials.
Apply flashmesh and coat with PCT36 to corners.
Apply two coats of PCT36 Slurry. Ligtly wet concrete before application.
Continue with priming and painting. PCT must be overcoated with UV resistant coating."

column1: 29,Wood Floors - Sanded to Renew & Varnish,m²,105,65,"• Note to Client: This is a dusty process. The complete floor must be done in one stage. The floor must be clear of furniture.
• Vacuum the floor to remove dust.
• Apply the first coat of indoor varnish thinned with 10% thinners to penetrate the wood.
• Sand the first coat with 300-grit sandpaper in circular movements and wipe clean.
• Apply the final coat. Second painter to lay off the wet varnish to prevent lines -maintaining a wet edge."
30,Wood Floors- Rails/ Decks Varnish,m²,45,55,"• Lightly sand wood to remove loose material and key in the new coat.
• Apply 2 coats Indoor Varnish for woodwork."

column2: Note to Client: Work following tile removal is a provisional part of the quote- Dependant on substrate condition the quote will have to be reassessed.
Larger cracks, imperfections or soft/powdery tops, may influence the cost of Materials. Provision is made is this quote for smaller cracks and imperfections.
This does not include skip rental, quoted under additionals
Remove tiles
Rake out all cracks and vaccuum to remove loose contamination
Use anchoring and patching cement to fill all cracks
Grind open expansion joints in concrete slab and seal with Skifa FC11"

column1: 32,Floors- Cup Grind (Prep),m²,30,45,"Note to Client: Cup grinding is a noisy and dusty process. We recommend to remove all items from the room. We aim to complete the work in the prescribed time.

column2: Throuogly sweep floor to remove loose conatminents.
Vaccuum floor to remove all dust and finer contaminents.
Pass the cup-grinder once over the area to achieve a level uniform top.
The objective is to remove the top layer of concrete to create a key coat for the following steps."

column1: 33,Floors- Cup Grind (Final),m²,75,90,"Note to Client: Cup grinding is a noisy and dusty process. We recommend to remove all items from the room. We aim to complete the work in the prescribed time.

column2: Throuogly sweep floor to remove loose conatminents.
Vaccuum floor to remove all dust and finer contaminents.
Pass the cup-grinder once over the area to remove high spots
Sweep and vacuum floor to remove all dust
Using a straight edge, look for high spots and mark with chalk
Pass over with the cup-grinder for a second time to cut a smooth uniform finish.
The objective is to grind down the top to a smooth, flat surface, which can be cleaned and sealed."

column1: 34,Floors- Pigmented Floor Cote,m²,750,150,"PREREQUISITES: All building work complete. Concrete min 25 MPa, moisture ≤ 10%. Minimum ambient temp: 15°C.

column2: Apply 1 coat Floor Screed Primer at ±8 m²/L. Allow 4 hours to dry.
Mix full 25 kg of Pigmented Floor Cote bag at once (liquid first, then powder) to a thick, lump-free paste. Must be applied in one continuous session – do not stop mid-section.
Trowel onto floor at ±3mm thick (4–5 m² per bag). Work towards yourself. Leave slight ridges or use sponge trowel for colour variation.
Allow 60–90 min to set. Lightly wet with block brush + plastic float (circular motion).
Finish with light steel trowel. Remove excess slurry immediately.
Allow 24–48 hours, sand with 200 grit; vacuum.
Apply 1–2 coats Colour Retaining Primer (10 m²/L, 4–6 hrs between).
Allow to dry overnight
Apply 2–3 coats Acrithane sealer"
Remove salt contamination with fresh water and Holdtight® 102
Sand and vacuum the floor thoroughly
Moisture content must be below 15%
Sound old coatings may be left in place
Remove all filters from spray equipment
Mix 504 Epoxy to 4:1, where 4 parts A and 1 part B.
Apply first coat of 504
Allow to dry overnight
Apply second Coat of 504 Epoxy
Allow 24 hours for final coat to cure before light use
Allow 7 days curing before full use- Do not put down any furnuture on the floor before this time, it will leave marks as Epoxy cures under the weight."
Apply 1 coat Floor Coat Screed Primer (D135) at ±8 m²/L
Apply 2 coats Tread FC Marble at max 1mm thickness per coat over 20–25 m² per 8 kg kit
Allow 4 hours between coats. Sand between coats and vacuum
Apply second coat in same manner. Sand and vacuum final coat before sealing.
Apply 1 coat Colour Retaining Primer at ±10 m²/L. Allow 4–6 hrs drying.
Apply 2 coats Acrithane sealer at ±10 m²/L per coat. Allow 8 hrs between coats. Full cure: 7 days (light traffic after 3 days)."
Apply D204 Epoxy Putty on all grout lines of tiles.
Cup grind tiles to remove the glaze to allow mechanical adhesion
Apply 1 coat of 504 Epoxy
While Epoxy cures (2 to 3 hours after application) apply 1 layer of GR.1 sand
Allow to cure overnight, sweep all loose sand
Apply 1 coat of Floor Screed Primer (D135) at ±8 m²/L
Apply Self-Levelling Screed primer to smooth floor
Sand to remove top laitance, vacuum thorougly
Apply 1 coat of Floor Screed Primer (D135) at ±8 m²/L
Close all windows and doors to prevent contamination
Sweep and vacuum floors
Apply 2 coats Tread FC Marble at max 1mm thickness per coat over 20–25 m² per 8 kg kit
Allow 4 hours between coats. Sand between coats and vacuum
Apply second coat in same manner. Sand and vacuum final coat before sealing.
Apply 1 coat Colour Retaining Primer at ±10 m²/L. Allow 4–6 hrs drying.
Apply 2 coats Acrithane sealer at ±10 m²/L per coat. Allow 8 hrs between coats. Full cure: 7 days (light traffic after 3 days)."
